body.title: DickSimnel: ReAct ToolLoop — multi-turn inference with tool execution
body.result: ToolLoop class + Bash/Read/Edit/Write dispatch + safety denylist; _run_inference() delegates to ToolLoop; 19 tests; committed
body.description:
DickSimnel currently does one inference call and posts the result as a ticket note. That makes it an analyst, not a worker — it can reason about a sprint ticket but cannot actually write files, run tests, or commit. This ticket adds a ReAct-style ToolLoop: a multi-turn inference loop where DickSimnel receives a tool result, incorporates it into its next inference call, and continues until it emits a DONE signal or hits a turn cap.

The loop enables DickSimnel to execute the sprint-ticket SKILL.md procedure step by step, just as CC does.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py (new), tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_toolloop.py (new)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first (ToolLoop is a class, not standalone functions); log every tool call at INFO per CLAUDE.md AR-009.

**Scope boundary:** In scope: ToolLoop class with Bash/Read/Edit/Write tool dispatch; turn cap (default 20); tool result injection into next inference message; loop termination on DONE_SIGNAL or exception. Out of scope: parallel tool calls, streaming output, tool permission gates (those are policy device work).

**Completion criteria:** tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_toolloop.py passes; DickSimnel._run_inference() calls ToolLoop and iterates tool calls until DONE; mock ToolLoop test shows multi-turn cycle with Bash call → result injection → second inference → DONE. Verified by running tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_toolloop.py green.
